title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Intrinsic Value
description: A comprehensive two-stage discounted cash flow (DCF) analysis of Broadcom Inc. (AVGO), calculating its intrinsic value based on projected free cash flows.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
Growth Rate (Years 1-5)

22.0%

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
Growth Rate (Years 1-5)

22.0%

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

items.title: AI Networking Deceleration
items.description: A significant portion of our 22% growth rate assumes hyperscalers continue their massive capital expenditures on AI infrastructure. If this spending cycle cools faster than expected, Broadcom's custom silicon and networking switch revenue will miss projections.

items.title: VMware Integration Hurdles
items.description: Broadcom heavily leveraged its balance sheet (adding over $40B in debt) to acquire VMware. If expected software synergies and margin expansions fail to materialize, the debt burden could restrict future cash flow and dividend growth.

items.title: Customer Concentration
items.description: Broadcom relies heavily on a few massive customers (notably Apple for wireless components and Google for custom ASICs). The loss of a major contract or a shift to in-house silicon by these titans would severely impair future cash flows.

faq.question: What is the intrinsic value of Broadcom (AVGO)?
faq.answer: Based on our two-stage DCF model, the intrinsic value of Broadcom (AVGO) is estimated at $213.54 per share. This suggests the stock may be overvalued relative to its current market price of $318.92.

faq.question: Why is the Free Cash Flow growth rate assumed to be 22%?
faq.answer: The aggressive 22% growth rate for the next 5 years is driven by explosive demand for AI networking infrastructure, custom silicon, and robust synergy realization from the VMware acquisition.

faq.question: How was the discount rate (WACC) calculated?
faq.answer: We utilized a discount rate of 9.2%, derived using the Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM). It factors in a 10-Year US Treasury Rate of 4.18%, Broadcom's beta, and a standard equity risk premium, slightly adjusted for their cost of debt.

faq.question: What is the terminal growth rate used in this analysis?
faq.answer: A terminal growth rate of 3.5% was applied. This slightly exceeds average long-term GDP growth, reflecting Broadcom's permanent structural advantages, dominant market share in networking, and incredibly high margin profile.
